title: Verification Definition
content: VERIFICATION = Build the Product RIGHT. Question: Are we building it correctly? Checks against: specifications; standards; guidelines. Performed by: BA Team; peer reviewers; QA team. Techniques: inspections; walkthroughs; peer reviews; checklists. Type: STATIC — no execution required. Occurs BEFORE validation in project lifecycle.
tags: verification definition static correct specification standard

title: Validation Definition
content: VALIDATION = Build the RIGHT Product. Question: Does it meet the actual need? Checks against: stakeholder needs; business goals; user expectations. Performed by: business stakeholders; end users; product owner. Techniques: prototypes; UAT; demos; scenario walkthroughs. Type: DYNAMIC — requires execution. Occurs AFTER verification in project lifecycle.
tags: validation definition dynamic right product stakeholder uat

title: Verification vs Validation Comparison
content: Verification: Build RIGHT; static; checks specs/standards; BA+QA team; inspections+walkthroughs. Validation: Build RIGHT product; dynamic; checks stakeholder needs; business users+PO; UAT+demos. Lifecycle timing: Requirements → Verification → Design → Build → Validation. States: Draft → Reviewed → Approved → Tested → Accepted. Both are required; neither alone is sufficient.
tags: verification validation comparison lifecycle timing static dynamic

title: Fagan Inspection - 6 Phase Process
content: Phase 1 PLANNING (moderator): select 10-15 pages; identify 3-6 participants; schedule overview+inspection meetings; distribute materials 2 days before; distribute checklists. Phase 2 OVERVIEW (author 30min): explain context; walk through structure; highlight complex areas; answer clarifying questions; confirm scope. Phase 3 PREPARATION (individual 2hr max): examine independently; use checklist; note defects with location+type; prepare questions. Preparation rate: 1 page per 10-15 min.
tags: fagan inspection phases planning overview preparation formal method

title: Fagan Inspection - Meeting and Closure Phases
content: Phase 4 INSPECTION MEETING (team 2hr max): moderator opens (5min ground rules); reader paraphrases requirements one by one; reviewers raise defects after each; recorder logs immediately. Rules: NO solutions discussed; NO personal attacks; author can clarify not defend; if stuck >2min move on. Rate: 1 page per 10-15 min. Phase 5 REWORK (author): fix all Critical+Major; defer Minor with documentation. Phase 6 FOLLOW-UP (moderator): verify fixes; decide Pass (<5% rework) or Re-inspect (>5% rework).
tags: fagan inspection meeting rework follow-up phases critical major

title: Fagan Inspection Roles and Responsibilities
content: Moderator: plans/facilitates/ensures process/decides pass-fail — must be senior BA or QA NOT the author. Author: created document/provides context/does rework — the BA who wrote it. Reader: paraphrases requirements during meeting — another BA (interpretation test). Recorder: logs all defects in real-time — any participant. Reviewer: examines/finds defects/provides expertise — SMEs/developers/testers.
tags: fagan inspection roles moderator author reader recorder reviewer

title: Structured Walkthrough Process
content: Purpose: author-led educational review. Formality: medium. Duration: 30-60 minutes. Participants: author (presenter) + 3-5 reviewers (peers/stakeholders). Process: (1) OPENING 5min — author states objectives/scope/ground rules; (2) PRESENTATION 30-45min — walk through section by section/explain rationale/answer questions; (3) DISCUSSION 10-15min — issues + suggestions + solutions CAN be discussed (unlike inspection); (4) WRAP-UP 5min — summarize action items/assign owners/determine follow-up.
tags: walkthrough author-led educational review process duration

title: When to Use Walkthrough
content: Use Structured Walkthrough when: early drafts needing feedback; complex requirements needing explanation; knowledge transfer to team; when stakeholder buy-in is important. NOT for final formal review (use inspection instead). Key difference from inspection: author IS the presenter and solutions CAN be discussed during meeting.
tags: walkthrough when to use early draft knowledge transfer stakeholder

title: Peer Review Overview
content: Purpose: quick quality check by colleague. Participants: author + 1-2 peers. Duration: 30-60 min async or 15 min sync. Formality: informal. When: before formal review; early drafts. Defect finding power: low compared to inspection. Best for quick checks and early drafts before investing in formal inspection.
tags: peer review quick check informal early draft colleagues

title: Review Technique Selection Guide
content: Inspection: defect finding 5/5; high time investment; best for critical documents/contracts/regulations. Walkthrough: defect finding 3/5; medium investment; best for knowledge transfer/early feedback. Peer Review: defect finding 2/5; low investment; best for quick checks/early drafts. Checklist Review: defect finding 3/5; low investment; best for consistency and completeness checks.
tags: review technique selection guide comparison inspection walkthrough peer

title: Boundary Value Analysis (BVA) Test Design
content: Principle: errors cluster at boundaries of input ranges. For each range generate 7 test cases: below-min (invalid); just-below-min (invalid); at-min (valid); normal-mid (valid); just-below-max (valid); at-max (valid); above-max (invalid). Example age 18-65: TC-001=17(invalid); TC-002=18(valid); TC-003=19(valid); TC-004=40(valid); TC-005=64(valid); TC-006=65(valid); TC-007=66(invalid).
tags: boundary value analysis bva test cases min max range invalid

title: Equivalence Partitioning Test Design
content: Principle: divide inputs into equivalence classes — one test per class. Example discount: <$50 (no discount); $50-99 (5%); $100-499 (10%); >=500 (15%). Generate one test case per partition. Combine with BVA: add boundary tests at $49/$50; $99/$100; $499/$500 for comprehensive coverage. Reduces test count while maximizing defect coverage.
tags: equivalence partitioning test design partition class discount

title: Decision Table Test Case Generation
content: For N binary conditions: 2^N test cases (one per rule column). Example: Premium Member (Y/N) + Order>$100 (Y/N) = 4 rules = 4 test cases. TC-001: Premium $150 → free shipping. TC-002: Premium $75 → free shipping. TC-003: Regular $150 → free shipping. TC-004: Regular $75 → $10 shipping. Each rule column maps directly to one test case.
tags: decision table test cases rules binary conditions coverage

title: UAT Lifecycle - 3 Phases Overview
content: Phase 1 PLANNING (2-4 weeks before): define scope/objectives; identify business users; establish entry/exit criteria; create schedule; prepare environment/test data; create UAT scenarios from requirements; train participants; set up defect tracking. Phase 2 EXECUTION (1-2 weeks): verify entry criteria; kick-off meeting; users execute scenarios; document Pass/Fail/Blocked; log defects; daily stand-up; re-test fixes. Phase 3 CLOSURE: verify exit criteria; summary report; sign-off; archive; lessons learned.
tags: uat lifecycle planning execution closure phases user acceptance

title: UAT Entry Criteria Checklist
content: All 9 must be true BEFORE UAT starts: all requirements baselined and approved; system testing completed with >=95% pass rate; all Critical/High defects from ST resolved; UAT environment deployed and verified; test data loaded and validated; UAT scenarios reviewed and approved; UAT participants trained; defect tracking system configured; UAT kick-off meeting completed.
tags: uat entry criteria checklist system testing defects environment

title: UAT Exit Criteria Checklist
content: Must be true BEFORE UAT closes: all UAT scenarios executed; >=95% of scenarios passed; all Critical defects resolved; all High defects resolved OR have approved workaround; no more than X Medium/Low defects open (define threshold upfront); UAT summary report completed; business sign-off obtained formally.
tags: uat exit criteria checklist sign-off scenarios passed critical resolved

title: UAT Scenario Template
content: Fields: SCENARIO ID; SCENARIO NAME; REQUIREMENT(S) linked (FR-xxx); PRIORITY; PRECONDITIONS. Steps table: Step|Action|Expected Result. Execution Log: Date|Tester|Result (Pass/Fail/Blocked). LINKED DEFECTS: DEF-xxx list. Minimum one happy path scenario + one error scenario per requirement. Results logged immediately during execution.
tags: uat scenario template steps execution log defects linked

title: Defect Type Classification
content: 8 types: MISSING — required info absent (no error message spec); WRONG — incorrect information (wrong formula); AMBIGUOUS — multiple interpretations (fast response); INCONSISTENT — conflicts with other reqs (contradicting rules); INFEASIBLE — cannot be implemented (100% uptime); UNVERIFIABLE — cannot be tested (user-friendly); DUPLICATE — same as another req; IRRELEVANT — out of scope (feature creep).
tags: defect type classification missing wrong ambiguous inconsistent infeasible

title: Defect Severity Levels
content: CRITICAL: blocks understanding; system unusable; resolution = fix IMMEDIATELY before next review. HIGH: major impact; workaround is difficult; resolution = fix BEFORE baseline within 24-48 hours. MEDIUM: moderate impact; workaround exists; resolution = fix before go-live within 1 week. LOW: minor issue; cosmetic; resolution = fix when convenient as backlog item.
tags: defect severity critical high medium low resolution timeline

title: Defect Priority Levels
content: P1 = Fix first — typically aligns with Critical/High severity. P2 = Fix next — Medium severity items. P3 = Fix if time permits — Low severity items. P4 = Defer to future — nice-to-have improvements. Priority sequence differs from severity: a High severity defect in low-priority feature may be P2; a Low severity defect blocking critical path may be P1.
tags: defect priority p1 p2 p3 p4 severity sequence

title: Defect Report Template
content: Fields: DEFECT ID; REPORTED BY; DATE FOUND; STATUS (Open/InProgress/Resolved); ASSIGNED TO. REQUIREMENT linked (REQ-ID + title). DOCUMENT + version + location (section/page/line). DEFECT TYPE (Missing/Wrong/Ambiguous/etc). SEVERITY (Critical/High/Medium/Low). PRIORITY (P1-P4). DESCRIPTION (clear specific). IMPACT (why it matters). SUGGESTED FIX (optional). RESOLUTION (how fixed/by whom/when). VERIFICATION (verified by/when).
tags: defect report template fields severity priority resolution verification

title: Verification Checklist - Completeness
content: COMPLETENESS checks per requirement: Has unique meaningful ID (e.g. FR-AUTH-001); has clear concise title max 10 words; description is complete (who/what/when/why); has testable acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then); source documented (stakeholder/meeting/document); priority assigned (MoSCoW or numeric); dependencies identified and valid; version and status current.
tags: completeness checklist id title description acceptance criteria source

title: Verification Checklist - Clarity
content: CLARITY checks: uses SHALL/SHOULD/MAY correctly per RFC 2119; no ambiguous words (fast/easy/user-friendly); only ONE possible interpretation; all terms defined in glossary; examples provided for complex requirements; acronyms defined on first use. If ANY clarity check fails: requirement must be rewritten before review passes.
tags: clarity checklist rfc2119 ambiguous interpretation glossary acronym

title: Verification Checklist - Correctness
content: CORRECTNESS checks: accurately represents stakeholder need (validated with stakeholder); business rules are factually correct; data values and ranges are accurate; all references to other reqs or docs are valid; calculations and formulas are correct. Validation method: trace back to original elicitation source (meeting notes/interview/workshop output).
tags: correctness checklist stakeholder business rules formulas references

title: Verification Checklist - Consistency
content: CONSISTENCY checks: no conflicts with other requirements (cross-checked); terminology used consistently throughout; follows document formatting conventions; aligns with project scope as defined; consistent level of detail across similar requirements. Tool: requirements conflict matrix — plot related requirements and check each pair for contradiction.
tags: consistency checklist conflicts terminology formatting scope detail level

title: Verification Checklist - Testability
content: TESTABILITY checks: can write specific executable test cases; pass/fail criteria are unambiguous; measurable metrics defined where applicable; edge cases and error conditions specified. A requirement IS testable if: metric exists; specific pass/fail defined; test case can be written before implementation; single responsibility; deterministic output.
tags: testability checklist metric pass fail test case executable

title: Verification Checklist - Feasibility
content: FEASIBILITY checks: technically achievable with available technology; within budget constraints; within timeline constraints; required resources and skills available; no unresolved TBDs remaining. If any feasibility check fails: escalate to project manager + sponsor. Document assumption and risk if proceeding with uncertainty.
tags: feasibility checklist technology budget timeline resources tbd risk

title: Verification Checklist - Traceability
content: TRACEABILITY checks: traces to business need or parent requirement; forward trace defined (design component + test case); rationale documented (why this requirement exists). Requirements with no trace = gold plating — remove or escalate. Requirements with no forward trace = risk of untested functionality.
tags: traceability checklist forward backward parent rationale gold plating

title: Requirements Set Completeness Checklist
content: Set-level checks: all scope areas covered; all user roles addressed; all functional requirements present; all non-functional requirements present; edge cases and exceptions documented; error handling specified for each function; security requirements addressed; reporting/analytics requirements addressed. Compare against use case list and actor list to find gaps.
tags: set completeness checklist scope roles functional nfr error security

title: Requirements Set Consistency Checklist
content: Set-level consistency: no contradicting requirements cross-checked (use conflict matrix); prioritization is consistent and defensible; terminology uniform across document; numbering scheme is consistent. Set-level organization: logically grouped by function/module; easy to navigate (table of contents/cross-refs); cross-references are valid and working.
tags: set consistency checklist conflicts priority terminology numbering

title: V&V Metrics - Core Formulas
content: Defect Density = Defects Found / Number of Requirements (target <0.5). Defect Removal Efficiency = Defects Found in V&V / Total Defects x 100% (target >85%). Review Coverage = Reviewed Reqs / Total Reqs x 100% (target 100%). Review Effectiveness = Critical+High Defects Found / Total Critical+High (target >95%). Cycle Time = Days from Draft to Approved (target <10 days).
tags: vv metrics formulas defect density removal efficiency coverage

title: V&V Metrics - Process Efficiency Formulas
content: Preparation Rate = Hours Prepared / Pages Reviewed (target 0.15-0.25 hr/page). Inspection Rate = Pages Reviewed / Meeting Hours (target 5-10 pages/hr). First-Pass Yield = Reqs Passing First Review / Total x 100% (target >80%). Rework Rate = Reworked Reqs / Total x 100% (target <20%). All 9 metrics should be tracked and reported per review session.
tags: metrics preparation rate inspection rate first-pass yield rework

title: Inspection ROI Calculation
content: Cost of Review = (Prep Hours + Review Hours) x Hourly Rate x Participants. Cost Avoided = Defects Found x Avg Cost if Found in Testing ($500 per defect). ROI = (Cost Avoided - Cost of Review) / Cost of Review x 100%. Typical ROI: 300-800%. Every $1 spent on review saves $3-8 in testing/rework. Industry benchmark: Capers Jones 6-8 defects found per review hour.
tags: inspection roi cost avoided review hours defects capers jones

title: CMMI Level 4 Review Effectiveness Benchmarks
content: Defects Found per Review Hour: target 4-8; Capers Jones average 6-8. Defect Detection Efficiency: target >80% (removes 80% of all defects). Cost of Finding per defect: target <$50 vs testing $500-1000. Pages per Hour (optimal): 5-10 (slower = more thorough). Prep Time Ratio: 0.5-1.0 hr per review hour (prep should equal review duration).
tags: cmmi benchmarks review effectiveness defect detection efficiency

title: CMMI Level 4 Process Capability
content: Cpk >= 1.33 for Defect Detection Efficiency = process is capable (CMMI Level 4 target). Control limits: UCL = Mean + 3sigma; LCL = Mean - 3sigma. Track monthly; investigate if outside control limits. Target 95% confidence interval for detection rates. Process out of control = data point beyond UCL/LCL = investigate root cause immediately.
tags: cmmi process capability cpk control limits defect detection

title: SRS Inspection Checklist - Correctness
content: CORRECTNESS: solution-independent (no HOW only WHAT)? Free from factual errors? Cross-references correct? Each requirement verified with stakeholder? Calculations and formulas accurate? Business rules reflect actual policy? Test: ask stakeholder to confirm accuracy of each requirement in review session.
tags: srs inspection checklist correctness solution-independent factual

title: SRS Inspection Checklist - Clarity and Structure
content: CLARITY: single interpretation only? Uniquely identified? Consistent detail level? STRUCTURE: interfaces defined? Inputs/outputs specified? Business rules documented? Quality attributes have metrics? No TBD/placeholder? All terms in glossary? Acronyms expanded on first use? Uses RFC 2119 keywords (SHALL/SHOULD/MAY) consistently?
tags: srs inspection checklist clarity structure interfaces glossary rfc2119

title: SRS Inspection Checklist - Feasibility and Verifiability
content: FEASIBILITY: achievable with current technology? Within resources and timeline? No contradictions with technical constraints? VERIFIABILITY: can be tested? Test criteria derivable from requirement? Measurable metric specified? No vague quality terms (fast/easy/good)? If all checks pass → requirement is ready for baseline. Fail on feasibility → escalate to project sponsor.
tags: srs inspection checklist feasibility verifiability testable metric

title: RFC 2119 Keywords in Verification
content: SHALL/MUST/REQUIRED = absolute requirement — failure to implement = non-compliance. SHALL NOT/MUST NOT = absolute prohibition. SHOULD/RECOMMENDED = strongly recommended; document reason if not implemented. SHOULD NOT/NOT RECOMMENDED = strongly discouraged; avoid unless valid reason. MAY/OPTIONAL = implement if resources permit; no penalty for omission. NEVER use: will/might/can/could (ambiguous obligation).
tags: rfc2119 shall must should may keywords obligation verification

title: Cross-Model Verification Procedure
content: Verify consistency ACROSS models: (1) Use Cases ↔ Data Model — every entity in UC must appear in ERD; (2) Process Model ↔ FR — every process step must have a corresponding functional requirement; (3) State Diagram ↔ FR — every state transition must map to a FR; (4) Business Rules ↔ Decision Tables — every rule in prose must have a row in decision table; (5) NFR ↔ Test Cases — every NFR must have a measurable test. Find gaps = missing requirements.
tags: cross-model verification consistency use case data model process

title: ISO 29148 Requirements Quality Attributes
content: ISO 29148 defines 8 characteristics for individual requirement quality: Necessary (traces to need); Appropriate (right level of detail); Unambiguous (one interpretation); Complete (all info present); Singular (one requirement only); Feasible (achievable); Verifiable (testable); Correct (accurate). For requirement set add: Consistent (no conflicts); Affordable (within budget); Bounded (within scope).
tags: iso29148 quality attributes necessary appropriate unambiguous

title: Requirements Audit Technique
content: Audit = systematic independent examination of requirements against standards. Different from review: audit uses external/senior reviewer not on project team. Audit checklist covers: compliance with organizational standards; use of approved templates; adherence to naming conventions; completeness of mandatory fields; correct use of RFC 2119 keywords; traceability matrix populated. Output: audit report with findings and corrective actions.
tags: audit technique independent examination standards compliance

title: Requirements Review Meeting Agenda
content: Agenda for 2-hour formal review: (1) 5 min — Moderator opens: objectives/ground rules/roles; (2) 5 min — Author context overview; (3) 90 min — Reader paraphrases; reviewers raise issues; recorder logs; (4) 10 min — Triage logged defects: Critical/Major/Minor; (5) 5 min — Decision: Pass/Conditional Pass/Re-inspect; (6) 5 min — Action items + owners + deadlines. No solutions in meeting.
tags: review meeting agenda moderator reader recorder triage decision

title: Pre-Baseline Requirements Quality Gate
content: Before baselining requirements check: Review completed (all reqs reviewed); Critical/Major defects resolved (zero open); TBD count = 0 (all resolved); Traceability = 100% (all reqs traced to business need); NFRs have measurable metrics (Planguage format); Glossary complete (all domain terms defined); Stakeholder sign-off obtained; Change control process activated. Any fail = delay baseline.
tags: baseline quality gate checklist sign-off traceability tbd zero

title: Defect Prevention vs Detection Strategy
content: Detection (reactive): inspection finds defects after writing. Prevention (proactive): write correctly first time. Prevention techniques: use EARS patterns (reduces ambiguity 30%); use decision tables for complex logic (prevents missing rules); use Gherkin acceptance criteria (forces testability); use Planguage for NFRs (forces measurability); peer review before formal inspection. Prevention ROI > Detection ROI.
tags: defect prevention detection strategy ears decision table gherkin planguage

title: Review Defect Density Benchmarks
content: Typical defect densities by review type: Inspection: 2-8 defects per page (most thorough). Walkthrough: 1-4 defects per page. Peer Review: 0.5-2 defects per page. If defect density in inspection >8 per page: document quality is poor → consider rewrite instead of review. If density <0.5 per page: document may be too high-level → consider splitting into detailed requirements.
tags: defect density per page benchmark inspection walkthrough peer review

title: UAT Test Scenario Quality Checklist
content: Each UAT scenario must have: linked requirement ID (FR-xxx); preconditions clearly stated; steps written from user perspective (not system); expected result per step (not just final result); test data specified (realistic not fictional); pass/fail criteria unambiguous; defect logging instructions; re-test procedure documented. Scenarios without linked requirements = gold plating — remove.
tags: uat scenario quality checklist linked requirement test data

title: Defect Escalation Decision Tree
content: CRITICAL: escalate to project manager immediately; stop review session; fix before proceeding. HIGH: log and assign owner; fix before next baseline; track daily. MEDIUM: log in backlog; fix before go-live; review weekly. LOW: log as improvement; fix when convenient; review monthly. Rule: if >3 Critical defects in one session → reject document → author rewrites → new review session required.
tags: defect escalation decision tree critical high medium low

title: Requirements Validation Techniques Comparison
content: Prototyping: high stakeholder engagement; catches missed requirements; expensive to build. UAT: real user validation; catches usability issues; requires working system. Document Review: cheap; fast; limited to specification errors. Simulation/Demo: visualizes behavior; good for complex workflows; may mislead on performance. Scenario Walkthrough: collaborative; catches logical gaps; stakeholder confirms mental model. Use multiple techniques — each finds different defect types.
tags: validation techniques comparison prototype uat review simulation scenario

title: Sign-off Checklist for Requirements Baseline
content: Before obtaining sign-off: all requirements reviewed and approved by designated reviewers; all Critical and High defects resolved; TBD count is zero; stakeholder review meeting held; open questions documented with resolution owners; known risks documented in risk register; change control procedure communicated to all parties; requirements management tool updated; version control applied. Get sign-off in writing.
tags: sign-off checklist baseline requirements approved risk change control
